lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: I've been posted here to guard this chasm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how's that going?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Not great. There's constant bursts of hot air, demon roars and strange lights coming from inside.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: And then there's the cultists. They just climb out like it's normal to be down there. Try to talk to you like it's business as usual.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That does sound stressful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long are you posted here?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Indefinitely. We don't know what the cult is up to and we don't want to be caught unprepared.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you considered quitting?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: No. I signed up to to protect Kourend, even if that means standing outside of a demon nest. Besides, it's not like any demons have come out of it yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, good luck.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: What do you want? Are you with those cultists?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? No.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Good. I've had enough of their games.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Games? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: They start off polite, but before you know it, they're trying to get you to agree to something.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: They're not fooling me though. The next one that crosses me is going to regret it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About the cultists... What sort of things do they ask you to agree to?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Usually they want you to follow them somewhere private. Some of the bolder ones try to get you to join them inside the chasm.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: If it were up to me, we'd launch a sortie right now. See how they like it if we come down on our own terms.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you worried about the demons?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: You sound like the lieutenant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sound eager to fight.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: I am. If you ask me we should be out fighting lizardmen. That means we need to go on the offensive and wrap this chasm business up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I take it the rest of your squad aren't as keen.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: You'd be right. The lieutenant is too cautious and the rest are too timid. The way some of them speak you'd think they had demon families.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: If you see any cultists lurking around, let me know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You look comfortable.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: I might as well be if we're going to continue this performance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: There are no demons coming out of this chasm. If there were, we wouldn't last long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You think the demons would overrun you?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Demons don't die the same way we do. We could hold back a few of them, but not an endless stream.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem to know a lot about them.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: I do, which means I know that guarding the chasm is a pointless endeavour. Not that the lieutenant cares what I think.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Launching an attack into the chasm would also be pointless. That leaves us with only one other option.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fleeing?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: No. We should talk to them. Try to reach an understanding.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somehow I don't think negotiating with demons will end well.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: If you have a better idea, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you a bit too calm about this?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: No. If they wanted to, Yama and his demons could overrun this little squad.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: The fact they haven't means they're probably not interested in doing so. As a matter of fact, they seem quite polite.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've spoken to them?

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: I didn't say that, But there's evidence to suggest that Yama and his cult can be reasoned with.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: If Lord Shayzien is interested in diffusing the threat, he should consider trying to reach an agreement with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've met Lord Shayzien. He doesn't seem like the type to compromise.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: You've met Lord Shiro Shayzien?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've met the leaders of all of the houses. Even King Artur Hosidius himself.

lines.speaker: Soldier
lines.text: Interesting. You're quite the worldly fellow. Perhaps King Hosidius would make a more level headed negotiator. Maybe you could talk to him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I imagine he's quite busy at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.
